experiment.name: stepwise_compression
experiment.description: Testing a step-by-step compression prompt that instructs the model to first draft a summary, then self-critique and compress further, producing only the final ultra-compressed version

diff.description: Added explicit two-pass self-compression instruction: draft a summary internally, then critique it for redundancy and produce only the final tighter version

agent_a.name: baseline_baseline_baseline
agent_a.system_prompt: You are a summarization assistant. When given text, produce the most concise summary you can while retaining all important details. Use precise language, eliminate filler words and redundancy, and ensure every sentence contributes unique information. Do not include unnecessary qualifiers or restate points already made.

agent_b.name: variant_v3
agent_b.system_prompt: You are a summarization assistant. When given text, mentally draft a summary capturing all key facts and relationships, then critically review your draft: cut any redundant phrasing, merge overlapping points, and tighten wording. Output only the final, compressed version. Use precise language, eliminate filler words and redundancy, and ensure every sentence contributes unique information. If removing a word or phrase would not lose meaning, remove it. Do not include unnecessary qualifiers or restate points already made.

tasks:
Summarize the following article:

The James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) launched on December 25, 2021, from Kourou, French Guiana, aboard an Ariane 5 rocket. It is the largest optical telescope in space, with a 6.5-meter primary mirror composed of 18 hexagonal gold-plated beryllium segments. The telescope orbits the Sun near the second Lagrange point (L2), approximately 1.5 million kilometers from Earth. JWST observes primarily in infrared wavelengths from 0.6 to 28.3 micrometers, using four main instruments: NIRCam, NIRSpec, MIRI, and FGS/NIRISS. The project cost approximately $10 billion and involved collaboration between NASA, ESA, and CSA over 20 years of development. Its sunshield, roughly the size of a tennis court, keeps instruments at operating temperatures below 50 Kelvin. Early observations have revealed previously unseen details of galaxy formation, exoplanet atmospheres containing CO2, and stellar nurseries in the Carina Nebula. The telescope is expected to operate for at least 10 years, with enough fuel potentially extending its mission to 20 years.

Summarize the following article:

The European Union's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), which took effect on May 25, 2018, replaced the 1995 Data Protection Directive and established comprehensive data privacy rules for all EU member states. The regulation grants individuals rights including data access, rectification, erasure ('right to be forgotten'), data portability, and the right to object to processing. Organizations must obtain explicit consent before collecting personal data, appoint Data Protection Officers (DPOs) if processing data at large scale, and report data breaches within 72 hours to supervisory authorities. Non-compliance can result in fines up to €20 million or 4% of annual global turnover, whichever is higher. GDPR applies extraterritorially to any organization processing EU residents' data, regardless of where the organization is based. Since implementation, regulators have issued over €4 billion in cumulative fines, with major penalties against tech companies including Meta (€1.2 billion for illegal data transfers), Amazon (€746 million), and WhatsApp (€225 million). The regulation has influenced similar legislation worldwide, including Brazil's LGPD, California's CCPA, and India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act.

Summarize the following opinion piece:

Remote work is not merely a pandemic-era stopgap — it represents a fundamental restructuring of the employer-employee relationship. Critics who demand full return-to-office ignore overwhelming evidence: Stanford economist Nick Bloom's research shows hybrid workers are 3-5% more productive than fully in-office peers, quit rates drop by 35%, and companies save an average of $11,000 per half-time remote worker annually. The real resistance comes from middle managers whose value proposition depends on visible oversight, and from commercial real estate interests desperate to justify trillion-dollar urban office portfolios. Yes, collaboration suffers in some forms — spontaneous hallway brainstorming is harder to replicate — but this romanticized view ignores how much office time is wasted in performative busyness, unnecessary meetings, and open-plan distractions. The companies thriving today are not those forcing rigid attendance policies but those redesigning work around outcomes rather than hours logged. The future belongs to organizations brave enough to measure what people produce, not where they sit while producing it.

Summarize the following step-by-step guide:

How to Set Up a Kubernetes Cluster on AWS Using EKS. Step 1: Install prerequisites — ensure you have the AWS CLI v2 installed and configured with appropriate IAM permissions (eks:CreateCluster, ec2:*, iam:CreateRole), kubectl v1.28+, and eksctl v0.167+. Step 2: Create the cluster by running 'eksctl create cluster --name my-cluster --region us-west-2 --nodegroup-name standard-workers --node-type t3.medium --nodes 3 --nodes-min 1 --nodes-max 5 --managed'. This provisions the control plane, VPC with public/private subnets, NAT gateway, and a managed node group. The process takes 15-20 minutes. Step 3: Verify connectivity with 'kubectl get nodes' — all three nodes should show 'Ready' status. Step 4: Install the AWS Load Balancer Controller by creating an IAM OIDC provider ('eksctl utils associate-iam-oidc-provider --cluster my-cluster --approve'), downloading the IAM policy JSON, creating the policy, and deploying via Helm chart. Step 5: Configure cluster autoscaler by deploying the autoscaler manifest and annotating the deployment with 'cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict: false'. Step 6: Set up monitoring by installing Prometheus and Grafana through the kube-prometheus-stack Helm chart ('helm install monitoring prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack'). Step 7: Implement RBAC by creating ServiceAccounts, Roles, and RoleBindings for different team members. Common troubleshooting: if nodes show 'NotReady', check the VPC CNI plugin logs; if pods are pending, verify resource quotas and node capacity.

Summarize the following narrative passage:

Maria had not spoken to her sister in eleven years, not since the argument over their mother's house — an argument that was never really about the house at all, but about who had stayed and who had left, who had changed bedpans and administered morphine at 3 a.m. while the other built a career in Vancouver. So when Elena's number appeared on her phone on a Tuesday afternoon in March, Maria let it ring four times before answering, her hand trembling not from age but from the sudden weight of a decade's silence. 'The cherry tree fell,' Elena said, as if no time had passed, as if they still spoke daily about the yard. Maria understood immediately — the tree their father had planted the year Maria was born, the one Elena used to climb to escape their parents' fighting, its branches a kind of neutral territory above the chaos. 'The roots were rotted through,' Elena continued. 'The arborist said it had been dying for years and we just couldn't tell from the outside.' They were both quiet then, letting the metaphor sit between them, neither willing to be the first to say what they were both thinking. 'I can come help clean up,' Maria finally offered. 'I'd like that,' Elena whispered.
